overview: This five-color Heroic deck leverages cheap creatures and instant-speed pump spells to generate rapid board presence and card advantage. By repeatedly targeting your own creatures, you trigger heroic abilities to draw cards and grow threats while maintaining tempo. The strategy focuses on efficient resource usage to outpace opponents before they can stabilize.
win_conditions: Overwhelming the opponent with pumped, high-toughness creatures
Generating card advantage through repeated heroic triggers
Using instant-speed pump spells as combat tricks to bypass blockers

keep_criteria: Hands containing one or two one-drop heroic creatures
Access to early pump spells like Practiced Offense
At least one removal or draw spell to sustain tempo
Keep any hand with a land and a Heroic creature (especially Illuminator Virtuoso or a cheap pump target).
Prioritize hands with 2-3 lands and at least one targeted spell like Practiced Offense or a combat trick.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with three or more lands
Hands lacking any creatures to target
Hands with only high-mana spells or late-game threats
Mulligan hands with only one color of mana, three+ lands with no early threats, or hands lacking a way to protect your early creatures (like Sheltered by Ghosts or similar protection).

early_game: Play cheap heroic creatures on curve
Use low-cost spells to trigger heroic and draw cards
Apply early pressure to force opponent responses
Turn 1: Play a land, ideally a fetch/shock or dual that enters untapped to set up five-color mana.
Turn 2: Cast a cheap Heroic creature, then immediately target it with a pump spell or combat trick to establish a 2/2 or 3/2 threat.
If holding Sheltered by Ghosts, consider holding it for protection against removal or to enable indirect targeting if the card's mechanics allow.
Sequence mana carefully to ensure you can cast both the creature and its first pump spell on curve.
mid_game: Pump key threats with Sheltered by Ghosts and Practiced Offense
Cycle through card advantage to maintain board presence
Use removal to clear blockers while preserving tempo
never run out of targeted spells unless you're at lethal.
Use mana efficiently by chaining pump spells onto the same creature to maximize value, but diversify threats to avoid being one-sweeper'd.
Manage your graveyard carefully if recursion is a threat
use exile effects or discard if needed.
Commit creatures when you have protection or enough pump spells to answer removal.
late_game: Leverage card draw to out-resource control strategies
Use combat tricks to close out games efficiently
Large, evasive Heroic creatures with multiple +1/+1 counters, often paired with unblockable effects or combat tricks.
Use Practiced Offense or similar late-game pumps to push lethal through blockers.
Go all-in when you have a clear path to lethal, the opponent has no sweepers, or you've established a board state they cannot clear with their remaining resources.
Commit when you can trade up favorably or when your life total is low enough that you must attack.

key_cards.name: Sheltered by Ghosts
key_cards.notes: Target a key creature to trigger heroic, give it +2/+2 and hexproof to survive opponent removal, then attack with it safely.

key_cards.name: Practiced Offense
key_cards.notes: Use as a combat trick to give a creature +3/+0 and first strike, triggering heroic while pushing lethal damage through blockers.

key_cards.name: Illuminator Virtuoso
key_cards.notes: Play early as a cheap target for spells to consistently draw cards, fueling your hand for more pump spells and removal.

sequencing_tips: Hold up pump spells until combat to maximize surprise and trigger timing
Prioritize targeting creatures that will survive the turn to avoid wasted triggers
Use Sheltered by Ghosts after opponent commits removal to protect your biggest threat
Sequence removal before pump spells if you expect opponent interaction
5c Heroic is highly dependent on spell density and mana consistency.
Avoid overcommitting to a single creature unless you have protection.
Be mindful of indirect targeting rules if your format supports them.
Practice stack interactions carefully, as Heroic triggers resolve on the stack and can be responded to.
Common mistake: pumping a creature to lethal but forgetting to account for combat damage or blocking, leading to unexpected losses.

matchups.advice: Play cheap creatures early and use pump spells to make blockers inefficient. Prioritize survival and card draw over trading.
Apply consistent pressure to prevent them from stabilizing. Use card draw to outlast their hand and pump threats to bypass sweepers.
watch_for: Hate cards like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria

matchups.advice: Race them with efficient pump spells and combat tricks. Use removal to clear their threats while maintaining your own board presence.

Use multiple cheap spells in a row to force counter spells, then pump the remaining creatures. Utilize indirect targeting if available, or rely on unblockable effects. Save protection spells for your key threats.

Apply early pressure to race their mana acceleration. Use removal or counterspells to disrupt their key ramp creatures. If they stabilize, switch to a control-oriented approach with targeted removal and sweepers.

Win by out-resourcing with better card draw and more efficient pump spells. Prioritize protecting your key threats with Sheltered by Ghosts or similar effects. Trade up favorably and use your sideboard cards to disrupt their spell density.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (like Wrath of God effects), hard removal (Path to Exile, Doom Blade), and graveyard recursion.
Note when opponents are holding up mana for counterspells or protection spells.
Track their life total and board state to decide between racing or stabilizing.
